answer:
The half cycle surge current rating is given for a duration of 10 ms (for 50 Hz), while one cycle corresponds to 20 ms. Surge current capability is inversely proportional to the square root of the pulse duration, so for double the time, the current is reduced by a factor of √2. Thus, 3000 A / √2 ≈ 2121.32 A.  

\boxed{A}
